name: Security First Bank
description_short: Community bank founded in 1898 offering personal and business checking, savings, loans, mortgages, and insurance services with a focus on customer relationships.
description_long: Security First Bank is a community bank with a long history dating back to its founding in 1898. The bank emphasizes relationship-based banking and has recently acquired First Interstate Bank accounts, expanding its customer base as of April 2026. The company positions itself as a traditional community bank committed to personalized service and local involvement. Security First Bank offers a comprehensive range of financial services including personal and business checking accounts, savings products, credit cards, home loans, and bridge loans for property transitions. They provide insurance services including umbrella insurance for liability protection. Digital banking features include eStatements, debit card controls for fraud prevention, real-time fraud alerts, and online account management. The bank also offers scholarship opportunities to support community members pursuing education. The bank distinguishes itself through its emphasis on community banking values, highlighting attention to detail, flexible services, and customer care as core pillars. They actively participate in community giving, as evidenced by their annual Feeding Hearts Food Drive (7th annual in 2026 with $18,300 donated). The recent First Interstate Bank acquisition demonstrates growth and expansion of their market presence, though this transition also represents significant operational change. As a traditional community bank, Security First Bank may have more limited digital innovation compared to neobanks, and specific details about account fees, interest rates, minimum balances, and loan terms are not provided on their homepage. Their strength lies in personalized service and local community connection rather than technological cutting-edge features.
services: Debit cards with card controls and fraud alerts
pros: Established institution with 128+ years of banking history and stability since 1898
Comprehensive service offerings including checking, savings, loans, mortgages, insurance, and credit cards all under one institution
Fraud prevention tools including real-time Card Controls, debit card alerts, and fraud protection resources
Digital convenience features like eStatements with enrollment notifications and online account access
Active community involvement demonstrated through annual scholarship programs and food drives with significant charitable donations
Specialized mortgage products including bridge loans for property purchase transitions
Personal insurance services including umbrella liability coverage available directly through the bank
cons: Website lacks specific pricing information such as account fees, minimum balance requirements, or interest rates for savings and checking products
Limited visibility into APRs for credit cards, personal loans, and mortgage products compared to competitors
Recent First Interstate Bank acquisition (April 2026) suggests ongoing integration that could impact service consistency during transition period
No information provided about digital banking app capabilities, mobile features, or online-only account options
Insufficient details about business banking services beyond brief mention in navigation menu
best_for: Community-oriented customers who value personal relationships and local banking presence over digital-first banking
Homebuyers needing bridge loans or traditional mortgage products with local service support
Small business owners seeking comprehensive banking and insurance services from a single provider
Customers prioritizing fraud protection tools and security-focused account management features
diagnosis: Security First Bank is best for customers who value established community banking relationships, comprehensive all-in-one financial services, and personalized customer support over cutting-edge digital features. Primary caveat: the website provides minimal pricing transparency on fees, rates, and terms, requiring direct contact with the bank for specific product details; additionally, the ongoing First Interstate Bank acquisition integration may affect service delivery during the transition period.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
meta_description: Community bank founded in 1898 offering personal and business checking, savings, loans, mortgages, and insurance services.
